title: Through the Dark Days: Self-Help as a Path out of Depression
description: This article explores the many facets of depression and offers a clear look at evidence-based treatments—including cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) and bibliotherapy—supported by research findings. It aims to equip readers with practical self-help tools and grounded hope for recovery, even in severe cases.

title: Depression Assessment: A Journey Toward Understanding Yourself
description: This article explores ways to assess depression—from recognizing common symptoms to self-evaluation using the Beck Depression Inventory (BDI). It emphasizes the importance of consulting professionals for an accurate diagnosis and support, especially when symptoms are pronounced, and offers practical steps and exercises for those who want to care for their emotional well-being.

title: “False” Depression: How Hormones and Vitamins Shape Emotional Health
description: This article looks at how physical health—especially hormonal balance and micronutrient levels—can shape our emotional state. It highlights that symptoms often associated with depression may actually be driven by medical issues such as vitamin deficiencies or endocrine disorders. The article emphasizes a comprehensive approach to assessment and care that takes into account both psychological and physiological factors.

title: Reframing Your Thoughts: Easing Depression by Understanding Cognitive Distortions
description: This article explains how cognitive distortions contribute to the onset and maintenance of depression—and why understanding these mechanisms matters. We offer practical ways to spot and shift negative thinking patterns that can improve how you feel. By looking more closely at cognitive distortions, you take an important step toward healing and strengthening your mental health.

title: Should Statements — Running in Shackles
description: This article explores how cognitive distortions shape our view of reality and affect mental health. We focus on understanding and loosening “should” statements to cultivate a more mindful, flexible, and joyful outlook.

title: Beyond Labels: The Art of Holistic Perception
description: This article examines the habit of labeling and explains how oversimplifying people—including ourselves—keeps us from seeing a person’s real complexity. We explore why labels constrain thinking, intensify negative emotions, and damage relationships, and we offer exercises that help cultivate a more holistic, nuanced view of people and the world.

title: Inside-Out Thoughts: Letting Go of Mind Reading
description: This article examines the cognitive distortion known as “mind reading,” our tendency to assume we know what others are thinking. It explains how this bias leads to mistaken beliefs about how people see us and how it affects our emotions and relationships. The piece ends with practical ways to counter this habit—including self-awareness and open communication—to improve mutual understanding and emotional well-being.

title: Self-Esteem in the Depths of Depression: A Path to Self-Acceptance and Inner Balance
description: This article examines how depression affects self-esteem, showing how negative self-perception intensifies depressive symptoms and stands in the way of a fulfilling life. Drawing on case examples and expert guidance, it offers practical steps to move beyond self-criticism and strengthen inner balance. It’s a practical guide for anyone seeking to build confidence and change their life for the better.

title: Technique: Stand Up to Your Inner Critic
description: This article presents effective, self-guided methods for strengthening self-esteem. The core technique is learning to counter your inner critic by keeping a structured table of self-critical thoughts, their cognitive distortions, and rational responses. The article emphasizes consistent practice to create meaningful shifts in self-perception and build self-esteem.

title: Technique: The Mirror of Self-Criticism
description: This practice helps you notice how harsh your inner critic can be. Through a short role-play, you redirect your usual self-critical phrases toward someone close to you and hear how they sound to someone else. The exercise teaches you to recognize the unfairness of self-devaluation and to develop a kinder attitude toward yourself.

title: Technique: Become Your Own Advocate
description: This technique offers a fresh way to overcome self-criticism by stepping into the role of your inner advocate. Using a courtroom analogy, you’ll learn to notice accusatory thoughts, challenge them, and defend yourself with arguments grounded in facts and good faith. The method helps cultivate a more supportive, rational, and respectful attitude toward yourself.

title: Perfectionism: When the Pursuit of the Ideal Becomes a Trap
description: Perfectionism is the drive toward an ideal that can become either your ally or your enemy. In this article, we look at maladaptive perfectionism—the kind that fuels self-criticism, fear of mistakes, and, as a result, depression.

title: Technique: Releasing the Fear of Mistakes
description: This article presents practical ways to address perfectionism by embracing imperfection and easing the fear of mistakes.

title: Awakening Your Will: Why Depression Breeds Apathy
description: This article explains how depression affects motivation and the ability to act. We examine why apathy arises, how inaction intensifies symptoms, and why it’s crucial to return to activity step by step—even when you lack energy or desire. You’ll find practical strategies to gradually restore vitality, motivation, and interest in life through movement, small steps, and a new perspective on what you do.

title: Technique: A Day in Control
description: This article presents a practical planning approach—the Two-Column method—designed to improve personal organization and boost effectiveness. You’ll see how a simple split between “Plan” and “What Actually Happened” can help you not only reach goals but also reflect on your day. Special attention goes to reviewing outcomes, which builds adaptability and balance between what must be done and what you want to do.

title: Technique: An Action Plan Against Procrastination
description: In this article, we reveal the secrets of combating procrastination using a proven anti-procrastination worksheet. Discover how simple steps—breaking tasks down and realistically rating their difficulty and the potential enjoyment—can transform the way you approach work and personal matters, boosting productivity and well-being.

title: The Shadow of Guilt: The Link Between Guilt and Depression
description: The article explores the connection between guilt and depression, highlighting how excessive or unfounded guilt can contribute to the development of depressive states. The authors distinguish between healthy and unhealthy guilt, noting that the former can motivate positive change, while the latter often leads to self-isolation and a deepening of depression. Strategies are offered to build self-awareness and address unhealthy guilt to improve emotional well-being.

title: Technique: A Path Out of the Trap of Unrealistic Expectations
description: This article addresses guilt that stems from unrealistic self-demands and constant self-criticism. It offers ways to rethink inner rules and replace “should statements” with a healthier view of your abilities and preferences. You’ll find practical tips to let go of self-blame and build a more balanced relationship with yourself and your mistakes.

title: What If My Problems Are Real?
description: This article discusses the difference between depression and natural sadness, emphasizing that depression is always linked to distorted thinking, while sadness is a normal response to real events. It clarifies that even serious life problems do not directly cause depression; they affect our emotional state through the filter of our thoughts. Understanding and correcting distorted thoughts can greatly help in coping with depression, separating it from natural sadness brought on by life’s circumstances.

title: Depression and a Terminal Diagnosis
description: This article examines the emotional and psychological challenges people face after receiving a serious diagnosis, highlighting how hard it can be to convey these experiences to people who are healthy. It tells the story of Olga, whose life changed abruptly after a suspected cancer diagnosis, and how she grappled with depression and loss of meaning through work with a psychologist. In the end, Olga recognizes her inherent self-worth and the importance of small accomplishments, which helps restore her self-esteem and shift her emotional state despite physical suffering.

title: How to Cope with Physical Limitations
description: This article explores how physical limitations affect emotional well-being, highlighting how cognitive distortions shape our sense of capability. Through the story of Anna—who faced depression after her husband’s disability—it traces a path toward emotional recovery using Cognitive Behavioral Therapy and a reevaluation of life values. The article closes by shifting the focus from limitations to possibilities, inviting readers to rethink what happiness and self-realization can look like.

title: Finding Yourself After Losing a Loved One
description: This article tells the story of Anastasia, who experienced profound depression and a heavy sense of guilt after her brother’s tragic death. Through psychotherapy, she gradually let go of self-blame and changed the way she related to herself and her experiences. Over time, Anastasia emerged from depression, accepted that she was not at fault, and began to live a full life again.

title: Losing Your Job—and Slipping into Depression?
description: This article explores the psychological and social impact of sudden job loss, tracing the emotional roller coaster that often follows. Through the story of Alexander, who spent years working at a successful company, it examines the struggle with depression, diminished self-esteem, and career setbacks. It also offers ways to move through these challenges, emphasizing the importance of support and self-respect during difficult times.

title: Feeling Sad Doesn’t Mean You’re Depressed
description: This article highlights the crucial difference between natural sadness and clinical depression. Loss, separation, hardship, and change are part of life—and feeling pain is a human response. We explain how to distinguish everyday emotions from depression, where distorted thinking plays a major role and professional help may be needed. The piece will help you understand your feelings and notice when sadness crosses the line.
